page: Amazon Web Services r5ad.8xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by AMD EPYC 7571 processors utilizing the x86_64 architecture. It features 32 virtual CPUs, corresponding to 16 physical cores with 2 threads per core, running at a clock speed of 2.5 GHz with dedicated CPU allocation. The instance is equipped with 256.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per vCPU. Storage is bundled as a 1200 GB local NVMe SSD instance store, and network connectivity is supported by an 8 Gbps baseline bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core CPU performance, measured via stress-ng and Geekbench, falls into the bottom 10% tier, and single-threaded compression and decompression tasks are similarly limited. However, multi-core CPU performance, Passmark CPU scores, and multi-threaded decompression scale to average levels in the middle 50% tier. Memory bandwidth tests for both small cached and large uncached blocks also register in the average tier.

Given its hardware profile, the r5ad.8xlarge is designed for memory-intensive applications that require high-capacity local scratch space but do not demand high single-threaded compute performance. The inclusion of 1200 GB of local NVMe SSD storage alongside 256 GB of RAM makes this instance qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads like in-memory databases, caching layers such as Redis, and data processing tasks where local storage speed and memory capacity are the primary bottlenecks.
description: Amazon Web Services r5ad.8xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 7571 processor running at 2.5 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 256.0 GB of DDR4 memory and a bundled 1200 GB local NVMe SSD instance store. While single-core CPU benchmarks and single-threaded compression tasks perform in the bottom 10% tier, multi-core execution, memory bandwidth, and database operations achieve average performance levels. With a baseline network bandwidth of 8 Gbps, this instance is designed for memory-intensive workloads that benefit from high-capacity local storage. It is well-suited for in-memory databases, caching layers, and distributed data processing tasks where memory capacity and local storage speed are prioritized over raw single-threaded compute power.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r5ad.8xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 32 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 1200 GB local NVMe SSD storage, designed for memory-intensive databases and caching workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r5ad.8xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 32 vCPUs, 256 GB DDR4 RAM, and a 1200 GB local NVMe SSD.
tagline: A memory-optimized instance combining high-capacity DDR4 RAM with fast local NVMe SSD storage for data-intensive and caching workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by AMD EPYC 7571 processors with 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores
Equipped with 256.0 GB of DDR4 memory and 1200 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Delivers average multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance
Features an 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Suited for in-memory databases, caching layers, and data processing
